channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: St. Patrick's Day parade celebrates Boston heritage in America’s most Irish big city
item.description: It’s that time of year again when more than a million revelers fill the streets of America’s most Irish big city for the South Boston St. Patrick’s Day parade.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Whittle, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump orders strikes on Iran-backed Houthi rebels in Yemen and issues new warning
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump said he ordered a series of airstrikes on the Houthi-held areas in Yemen on Saturday, promising to use “overwhelming lethal force” until Iranian-backed Houthi rebels cease their attacks on shipping along a vital maritime corridor.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, SAMY MAGDY and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: SpaceX mission to replace stuck NASA astronauts launches
item.description: A Falcon 9 rocket carrying the Crew-10 successfully launched from NASA's Kennedy Space Center at 7:03 p.m. ET.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump signs order to gut staff at Voice of America and other US-funded media organizations
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration on Saturday began making deep cuts to Voice of America and other government-run, pro-democracy programming, with the organization's director saying all VOA employees have been put on leave.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI and DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump invokes Alien Enemies Act in mass deportation effort
item.description: The president’s move comes hours after a judge temporarily blocked the removal of 5 Venezuelans who the Trump administration says are members of Tren de Aragua.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Here's what you need to know about St. Patrick's Day
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — If it's March, and it's green, it must be St. Patrick's Day. The day honoring the patron saint of Ireland is a global celebration of Irish heritage.
item.dc:creator: DEEPTI HAJELA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bulls title banners removed for season after sustaining damage during Disturbed concert
item.description: All six Bulls championship banners have been removed from the United Center for the rest of the season after sustaining damage during a concert.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, Fox 5

item.title: Cybersecurity officials warn against potentially costly Medusa ransomware attacks
item.description: The FBI and the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency are warning email users against a dangerous ransomware scheme.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Parvini, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Search goes statewide for missing Florida teen Allister Nasr
item.description: On Friday, Nasr's family was knocking on doors and handing out flyers near where police said they found Nasr's bike on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Marilyn Parker, NewsNation

item.title: The 49ers bring back Kyle Juszczyk days after cutting him, AP source says
item.description: Fullback Kyle Juszczyk is returning to the San Francisco 49ers on a two-year, $8 million contract just days after being cut, a person familiar with the deal told The Associated Press on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The line-swapping hack at Disney, Universal and Six Flags that all parents should know about
item.description: Are some of your children too short, too young, or just too terrified to accompany you on your favorite theme park rides? If so, it doesn't have to slow you down.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KREX 5

item.title: Missing American student: Dominican lawyer puts drowning theory back in play; same beach claimed 4 in January
item.description: A Dominican lawyer believes the possibility that missing Pitt student Sudiksha Konanki drowned is more plausible than some experts have indicated.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Big March storm fans wildfires and kills 3 as forecasters fear weekend tornado outbreak
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — A sprawling storm system crossing the U.S. on Friday caused deadly crashes, damaged buildings and fanned more than 100 wildfires in several central states, prompting evacuation orders in some communities.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY and RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Yolanda Saldívar thinks Selena 'partly to blame' for death: Report
item.description: Saldívar, 64, officially filed for parole in January. She shot Selena during a confrontation in a Texas hotel room.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Banfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Aircraft catches fire after landing in Denver, sending passengers onto wing as smoke engulfs plane
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A fire on an American Airlines plane after it diverted mid-flight and landed at Denver International Airport sent passengers fleeing onto a wing in a fraught evacuation amid billowing clouds of smoke. Airport officials said 12 people were taken to hospitals with minor injuries.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS PEIPERT, SARAH BRUMFIELD, JESSE BEDAYN, COLLEEN SLEVIN and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tampa's Doechii joins Will Smith to recreate viral TikTok trend
item.description: A collaboration decades in the making, Grammy-award winning rapper Doechii joined "Fresh Prince of Bell-Air" alums Will Smith and Tatyana Ali to recreate a viral TikTok trend.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Fanning, WFLA, NBC

item.title: SpaceX mission to replace stuck NASA astronauts launches
item.description: A Falcon 9 rocket carrying the Crew-10 successfully launched from NASA's Kennedy Space Center at 7:03 p.m. ET.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, KTLA 5

item.title: Senate votes to avoid government shutdown
item.description: The Senate voted mostly along party lines Friday afternoon to pass the House Republican-drafted bill to fund government through September, avoiding a government shutdown only hours before funding was due to lapse. President Trump is expected to sign the bill into law. The final vote was 54-46.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: More than 50 universities face federal investigations as part of Trump's anti-DEI campaign
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More than 50 universities are being investigated for alleged racial discrimination as part of President Donald Trump’s campaign to end diversity, equity and inclusion programs that his officials say exclude white and Asian American students.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Viral 'American Idol' contestant dead at 32, family says
item.description: Douglas Kiker, the garbage collector who touched hearts with his singing voice on "American Idol" has died, his family announced on social media. He was 32 years old.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration moves to dismiss lawsuits against Iowa and Oklahoma over immigration laws
item.description: The Trump administration has moved to dismiss lawsuits against Iowa and Oklahoma for their immigration laws.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Fingerhut, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hundreds of federal offices may start shutting down this summer due to DOGE
item.description: Federal agencies will begin to vacate hundreds of offices across the country this summer under a frenetic and error-riddled push by Elon Musk’s budget-cutting advisers to terminate leases that they say waste money.
item.dc:creator: By RYAN J. FOLEY, JOSHUA GOODMAN and CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Air taxis flying over Austin could transform future commutes
item.description: "You can come and train in VR and in less than an hour you can solo fly vertical lift aircraft," Chasen said.
item.dc:creator: Eric Henrikson, NewsNation

item.title: Newborn kittens rescued from wrecked car in New York moments before demolition
item.description: A litter of kittens was rescued from the inside of a wrecked car at a junkyard moments before it was scheduled to be demolished Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, KRON4

item.title: Trader Joe's recalls 61K sparkling water bottles
item.description: Trader Joe's announced a recall of two lots of the popular Gerolsteiner Sparkling Natural Mineral Water, a non-Trader Joe's branded item, which the grocery giant sells.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, Fox 5

item.title: John Feinstein, one of country's foremost sports writers, dies suddenly
item.description: John Feinstein, one of the country's foremost sports writers and the author of numerous bestselling books, including the groundbreaking “A Season on the Brink” about college basketball coach Bob Knight, died unexpectedly Thursday. Feinstein was 69.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Man describes cruelty during his two decades of captivity at his family home in Connecticut
item.description: A man rescued from a burning home in Connecticut told a shocking story of cruelty and constant hunger as he was held captive in a single room for 20 years by his father and stepmother, according to a newly released arrest warrant.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Khalil protestors fill up Trump Tower
item.description: Protesters opposed to the detention of Palestinian activist Mahmoud Khalil filled up Trump Tower in New York on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, KRON4

item.title: Pete Buttigieg declines Senate run in Michigan
item.description: Former Transportation Secretary Pete Buttigieg announced he will not run for Senate in Michigan, keeping the door open for a potential presidential run in 2028.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, WGN 9

item.title: FAFSA site goes down after mass layoff at Education Department
item.description: The Studentaid.gov website was down for hours on Wednesday after the Education Department laid off half its workforce.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A breakdown of major EPA deregulatory moves around water, air, climate
item.description: Environmental Protection Agency Administrator Lee Zeldin on Wednesday announced nearly three dozen deregulatory moves that he said would spur the U.S. economy by rolling back rules that have unfairly burdened industry.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PHILLIS, ALEXA ST. JOHN and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 4-year-old calls 911 to say 'bad mom' ate his ice cream and needed to go to jail
item.description: We've all been there. You go to the freezer and discover your ice cream has been eaten, and not by you.
item.dc:creator: Laura Morrison, Fox 5

item.title: What to know about US student Sudiksha Konanki, who vanished in Dominican Republic on spring break
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — Investigators continue to search for a college student from Virginia who vanished earlier this month while visiting the Dominican Republic with five other people during spring break from classes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Education Department layoffs gut its civil rights office, leaving discrimination cases in limbo
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Education Department’s civil rights branch is losing nearly half its staff in the Trump administration’s layoffs, effectively gutting an office that already faced a backlog of thousands of complaints from students and families across the nation.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Search ends for Dane Paulsen after volunteer diver recovers body
item.description: Dane Paulsen, a 2-year-old boy, was found dead in an area near his family home in Siletz, Ore., after a massive search effort spanning weeks.
item.dc:creator: Ariel Iacobazzi, WFLA, NBC

item.title: EPA likely to move to further limit federal protections for wetlands
item.description: The Trump administration on Wednesday announced it will reconsider the reach of the nation’s bedrock clean water law and likely further limit the wetlands it covers, building on a Supreme Court decision two years ago that removed federal protections for significant areas.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration drops lawsuit against company over alleged abuse at its child migrant shelters
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — The Trump administration moved to drop a civil lawsuit Wednesday against the largest provider of housing for migrant children over allegations of sexual abuse and harassment of unaccompanied minors, saying it also would no longer be using that company's services.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4
